reasoning: The FRED trend analysis estimates a 0.59 probability of an increase, driven by a slight upward linear trend over the last 30 days and historical seasonal strength in August (where last year saw prices around $78). Therefore, I anchor my short-term estimate near this value at 0.57 for August 10. However, medium-to-long term predictions from structural analysts are more bearish (Goldman Sachs forecasted $56 for 2025, and others predicted $60-65 earlier in the year), expecting strong non-OPEC+ supply and shaky global demand. Acknowledging this broader bearish context and adhering to the rule that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, I regress the probability steadily toward 0.5 for subsequent, more distant resolution dates (September, November, and January).
